process_name: Lepton-flavor-violating Z decay Z -> e mu
implementation_difficulty_reason: MEDIUM to store the external branching-ratio upper limit, but HIGH for production-grade RS integration because the repo lacks LFV Z-coupling operators, electroweak/lepton-sector matching, and a policy for combining direct Z-pole and low-energy mu-to-e constraints.

evidence: Targeted grep for Z -> e mu / Z -> mu e / Zemu / ZeMu / LFV Z strings across required code directories returned no hits.
The only charged-lepton LFV implementation found is mu -> e gamma: flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75.
The scan calls the mu -> e gamma LFV filter at scanParams/scan.py:524.
The modern phenomenology policy surface is limited to epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0: quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23.
grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|evaluate_d0_mixing|check_mu_to_e_gamma" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "Z.?->.?e.?mu|Z.?->.?mu.?e|Z.?to.?e.?mu|Z.?to.?mu.?e|Zemu|ZeMu|e.?mu.*Z|mu.?e.*Z|lepton.?flavou?r.*Z|LFV.*Z" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(Z\to e^\pm\mu^\mp)\).
This entry covers the charged-lepton-flavor-violating on-shell \(Z\)-boson
decay into one electron and one muon, summed over charge-conjugate final
states, in the top/Higgs/electroweak family.
sections.pdg_prose:
The source-of-truth values are in the \texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent} block of
\texttt{T015.yaml}.  The sidecar-selected experiment-equivalent headline value
is the CMS 2025 direct-search result
\[
  \mathcal{B}(Z\to e^\pm\mu^\mp) < 1.9\times 10^{-7}
\]
with an expected limit of \(2.0\times 10^{-7}\), at 95\% CL, using
\(138~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) of \(\sqrt{s}=13~\mathrm{TeV}\) data
(\texttt{CMS2025ZLFV}; \texttt{CMS2025:T015:zemu\_limit};
\texttt{CMS2025:T015:dataset}).
The PDG 2025 \(Z\)-boson listing quotes the charge-summed limit
\[
  \mathcal{B}(Z\to e^\pm\mu^\mp) < 2.62\times 10^{-7}
\]
at 95\% CL (\texttt{PDG2025:T015:zemu\_limit}), matching the ATLAS Run-2
search with \(139~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) at \(\sqrt{s}=13~\mathrm{TeV}\)
(\texttt{PDG2025ZLFVListing}; \texttt{ATLAS2023ZLFVEmu};
\texttt{ATLAS2023:T015:zemu\_limit}; \texttt{ATLAS2023:T015:dataset}).
For historical comparison, the earlier ATLAS Run-1 search set
\(<7.5\times 10^{-7}\) with \(20.3~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) at
\(\sqrt{s}=8~\mathrm{TeV}\) (\texttt{ATLAS2014ZLFVEmu};
\texttt{ATLAS2014:T015:zemu\_limit}; \texttt{ATLAS2014:T015:dataset}).
sections.relevance:
Anarchic or partially composite lepton sectors can induce non-diagonal neutral
currents after rotating to charged-lepton mass eigenstates.  A direct
\(Z\to e\mu\) limit tests any effective off-diagonal \(Z e\mu\) coupling from
KK gauge mixing, zero-mode coupling non-universality, or lepton-sector flavor
symmetry breaking.  It is therefore relevant to a lepton/electroweak extension
of the present pipeline, not to the existing quark \(\Delta F=2\) scan lane.
sections.post_2008:
The arXiv:0804.1954 warped-flavor baseline predates the LHC direct
\(Z\)-LFV program (\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008WarpedFlavor};
\texttt{PerezRandall2008WarpedLeptonFlavor}).  Since then ATLAS established
the \(8~\mathrm{TeV}\) \(e\mu\) search at the \(10^{-7}\) level
(\texttt{ATLAS2014ZLFVEmu}), then improved it with the full Run-2 data set
(\texttt{ATLAS2023ZLFVEmu}); CMS subsequently pushed the direct \(e\mu\)
limit below \(2\times10^{-7}\) (\texttt{CMS2025ZLFV}).  On the theory side,
post-LEP EFT studies of future Tera-\(Z\) facilities emphasize that indirect
low-energy \(\mu\)-to-\(e\) constraints can make \(Z\to \mu e\) harder to
observe than \(Z\to\tau\ell\) even with \(\mathcal{O}(10^{12})\) produced
\(Z\) bosons
(\texttt{CalibbiMarcanoRoy2021:T015:teraZ}).
sections.validity:
Experimentally this is a clean direct upper limit on a forbidden Standard
Model decay.  Its use as a scan cut assumes SM-like inclusive \(Z\) production
and total \(Z\) width.  Model interpretations are less universal: in many
lepton-flavor models the same operator structure is correlated with
\(\mu\to e\gamma\), \(\mu\to3e\), or coherent \(\mu\)-to-\(e\) conversion,
so this should be treated as a direct collider constraint on \(Z e\mu\), not
as a complete charged-lepton flavor-violation likelihood.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
Coverage is \textbf{NO}.  The required broad greps and a targeted search for
\texttt{Z -> e mu}, \texttt{Z -> mu e}, \texttt{Zemu}, and related LFV-\(Z\)
strings across \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/} found no \(Z\to e\mu\) observable implementation.  The only
charged-lepton LFV backend seen is \(\mu\to e\gamma\)
(\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}), wired into the scan at
\texttt{scanParams/scan.py:524}; the modern phenomenology policy surface is
limited to \(\epsilon_K\), \(K\), \(B_d\), \(B_s\), and \(D^0\)
(\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}).
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
Production integration is \textbf{HIGH}.  Storing the external limit is
straightforward, but a faithful RS constraint needs a new electroweak LFV
operator convention, matching from lepton localization and gauge/Higgs-sector
mixing to \(Z e\mu\), and a decision on how to combine direct \(Z\)-pole
limits with low-energy \(\mu\)-flavor observables.

priority_rationale_short: Clean Z e mu LFV limit, but muon LFV dominates
priority_rationale_full: The Z -> e mu bound is a clean direct collider limit on an off-diagonal electroweak lepton coupling. It matters for lepton-bulk or partially composite lepton extensions because it tests a different observable than dipole searches. The global e-mu LFV constraint is usually dominated by mu -> e gamma, mu -> 3e, and mu-to-e conversion, so this is MEDIUM rather than HIGH.
